label: Uncomplicated acute otitis media without mastoiditis concern
inclusion_criteria: Clinician diagnosis or discharge impression is acute otitis media, otitis media, or middle ear infection.

No mastoiditis, deep ear infection, eardrum perforation, tympanostomy tube complication, ear trauma, foreign body, otitis externa pathway, acute hearing loss, facial weakness, or severe systemic illness documented.
No specialist-directed ENT plan or individualized immunocompromised-host pathway.
exclusion_criteria: Mastoiditis, deep ear infection, skull base infection, or severe systemic illness concern.
Perforated eardrum, tympanostomy tube, ear trauma, ear foreign body, acute hearing loss, vertigo, facial weakness, or specialist-directed ENT plan.
Otitis externa or ear canal infection pathway.
Immunocompromised host, frail elderly patient, recurrent or chronic ear infection, or pediatric dosing pathway requiring individualized instructions.
default_follow_up: Follow up with primary care, urgent care, or an ear clinician as instructed, especially if symptoms are not improving.

source_audit.notes: MedlinePlus and CDC support acute otitis media/middle-ear infection framing, ear pain and fever symptom education, clinician-directed observation or antibiotic decisions, and escalation for severe or worsening illness. These sources do not independently support ED-specific risk stratification, so runtime must require a clinician diagnosis and block mastoiditis, perforation, tube, otitis externa, trauma, foreign body, immunocompromise, and systemic illness concerns.

terms: no perforation tube trauma foreign body hearing loss facial weakness

review.notes: Phase 155 draft only. Acute otitis media is source-supported for patient education, but antibiotic choice, watchful waiting, pediatric dosing, and mastoiditis boundary require EM clinician review before promotion.
